scenario: Public README, docs, release notes, and issue updates stay inside the proven beta boundary and avoid unsupported product claims.
pass_criteria: Docs describe local Codex orchestration as beta and approval-gated.
Unsupported adapters are named as experimental or stubbed.
Release text records remaining blockers instead of converting them into proof.
Forbidden actions are recorded as not performed unless explicitly approved.
fail_criteria: Docs imply unsupported adapter parity, remote sync, autonomous desktop control, permission bypass, or enterprise security proof.
Issue or release text claims live control proof without explicit approved smoke evidence.
Public evidence includes private local data or secrets.
known_gaps: Claim audit needs periodic expansion as new docs and adapters are added.
Human release wording review remains required before public beta announcement.
next_action: Re-run claim audit tests and compare README/docs/release notes against VISION.md before public beta publish or release copy changes.
proof_boundary: Scored as a public-safe beta claim review for current repo README, VISION, release-gate tests, and issue-update wording through issue #278. It does not certify future release notes, npm latest promotion, 1.0 stability, customer readiness, or human announcement copy.
